project_type: Electron desktop app with LitElement web components
language: JavaScript (ES modules in renderer, CommonJS in main process)
verification_instructions: 1. Run npm install to ensure dependencies resolve. 2. Run npx prettier --check src/ to verify formatting. 3. Verify no syntax errors by running node -e "require('./src/index.js')" (checks main process modules parse correctly).
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access. No test suite exists. Verification is limited to syntax checks and prettier.
contribution_requirements: Run npx prettier --write . before committing. Use .prettierrc settings (4-space indent, 150 print width, semicolons, single quotes).
key_patterns: Main process (src/index.js, src/utils/window.js, src/storage.js) uses CommonJS require(). Renderer (src/utils/renderer.js) uses require('electron').ipcRenderer directly (nodeIntegration=true, contextIsolation=false). UI components use LitElement from vendored lit-core-2.7.4.min.js. Global cheatingDaddy object exposes storage/window/theme APIs to components. IPC pattern: main registers handlers via ipcMain.handle(), renderer wraps them in the storage/windowControls objects. Window state persisted in window-state.json via storage.getWindowState()/setWindowState(). Preferences persisted in preferences.json. Theme system in renderer.js applies CSS variables. Hotkeys registered as globalShortcuts in window.js, emit IPC events to renderer (e.g. 'scale-changed', 'zoom-changed', 'opacity-changed', 'font-size-changed').
directory_structure: src/index.js (main process entry), src/utils/window.js (window creation + global shortcuts), src/utils/renderer.js (renderer process globals + IPC wrappers + theme + cheatingDaddy object), src/storage.js (filesystem persistence), src/components/app/CheatingDaddyApp.js (root LitElement), src/components/views/ (page views as LitElement components)
